# Security Policy

## Reporting a vulnerability

Please do not report security vulnerabilities through public GitHub issues, discussions, or pull requests.

If you believe you have found a security issue, use GitHub's private vulnerability reporting for this repository.

Include as much of the following as possible:

- A short description of the issue and its impact
- Steps to reproduce
- A proof of concept, sample markup, or affected configuration
- Any known affected versions or environments
- Suggested mitigations, if you have them

## What to expect

- Initial acknowledgement is typically expected within a reasonable best-effort timeframe.
- Reports will be reviewed to confirm whether the issue is valid and security-relevant.
- If the report is accepted, a fix will be prepared and released when practical.
- You may be asked for additional reproduction details to help verify the issue.

## Disclosure guidance

Please give the maintainer reasonable time to investigate and address the issue before making details public.

Once a fix is available, coordinated disclosure is appreciated.
